pages.summary:
This notice is issued on behalf of Resolv Digital Assets Ltd. in relation to the Resolv protocol.
Earlier today, a malicious actor gained unauthorized access to Resolv infrastructure through compromised private key, resulting in the minting of approximately $80M of…
— Resolv Labs (@ResolvLabs) March 22, 2026 Once again its proven by the black hats that they don’t care how secure your codebase is or how many audits you have done.
There was no reentrancy. No oracle manipulation. No overflow hiding in an unchecked block. The contracts behaved exactly as designed. And yet, funds were drained.
pages.title: Web3’s Real Attack Surface: Beyond Smart Contracts

pages.summary:
Introduction On ethereum, every transaction enters hostile environment; a place often called as Dark Forest. Before execution, transactions sit in a public mempool where automated agents/bots continuosly monitor, simulate and compete with each other to extract profit. These so called MEV bots don’t wait for mistakes. They exploit them instantly.
Recently, a ~$50M swap involving Aave exposed just how dangerous this environment an be. What a appeared to be a routinely daily trade from someone resulted in catastrophic slippage, routing through a pool with negligible liquidity and leaving trader with fraction of expected amount.
pages.title: Being Safe in the Dark Forest

pages.summary:
Smart contract exploits rarely come from obvious code paths. They emerge from unexpected edge case sequences of state transitions that traditional tests never explore. Most of the smart contracts today rely heavily on unit tests. Unit tests validate correctness along expected flows. Attackers don’t follow expected flows. This is where fuzzing becomes indispensable.
In this article, we’ll walk through how to write real, invariant-driven fuzz tests using Trident, a purpose-built fuzzing framework for Solana programs.
pages.title: Fuzz solana programs like a Pro - Part 1

pages.summary:
“Thinking is difficult, that’s why most people judge.” - Carl Jung
I don’t know if this quote is relevant for this post or not but one thing I know for sure is that technology has changed our way of thinking in every way. What is thinking? Thinking is process of consciously using the brain and responding to the world from your reactions to it. Your thinking frame of reference is different from others. It is defined by point of view or perspective, a frame of reference is shaped by our prior knowledge, assumptions values or language, about others. Our frame of reference influence our way of thinking in much broader way. It is important to know that frame of reference can change of way of decision making and thinking.
pages.title: Technology, thinking and your dumb brain

pages.summary:
You might be wondering in what sense I have kept this title? But right now this is what is going on everywhere. Millions of people are getting betrayed by the social media and companies and we just don’t care. Internet is big free playground you might say but what is really going behind the scenes is different story today.
Today on almost every site you see those “Click on I agree” boxes. Blindly you just share your privacy with them. …. Like wait what are you trying to say? You must have heard about Facebook Cambridge Analytica scandal which exposed almost 87 million profiles for Trump campaign in 2016. If that’s not enough then on September 25, 2018 another potential data leak of 50 million users happened. And this is not related to Facebook only, Amazon, Google, Netflix, etc. are also involved in this privacy related issues.
pages.title: Internet, Privacy and How Dumb YOU Are..!
